title: crataegus crus-galli f. inermis
intro: Crataegus crus-galli f. inermis, also known as the Cockspur Hawthorn, is a deciduous shrub or small tree belonging to the Rosaceae family. The plant is used in traditional medicine for its potential to support cardiovascular health, thanks to its rich content of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other bioactive compounds. Crataegus crus-galli f. inermis has been traditionally used to treat conditions such as high blood pressure, heart failure, and angina, as well as to improve circulation and reduce inflammation. The plant can be prepared as a tea, decoction, or tincture, and its medicinal properties have been utilized in various herbal preparations to promote overall well-being and protect against oxidative stress.
